statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be in the format: first line representing the number of vertices, and the second line representing the adjacency list of the graph represented as an undirected graph.
statement.outputFormat: Return 1 if the given graph is isomorphic to the other given graph, otherwise return 0.
statement.notes: This is a simple case of graph isomorphism.

hints.text: Try to visualize the problem first

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int isIsomorphic(int n, vector<vector<int>>& adj) { int cnt = 0; for (int i=0; i<n; i++) { for (int j: adj[i]) { if (i > j) return 1; } } return 0; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: import java.util.List; public class Solution { public int isIsomorphic(int n, List<List<Integer>> adj) { int cnt = 0; for (int i=0; i<n; i++) { for (int j: adj.get(i)) { if (i > j) return 1; } } return 0; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def isIsomorphic(self, n: int, adj: list[list[int]]) -> int: cnt = 0; for i in range(n): for j in adj[i]: if i > j: return 1; return 0
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function isIsomorphic(n, adj) { var cnt = 0; for (var i=0; i<n; i++) { for (var j of adj[i]) { if (i > j) return 1; } } return 0; }
